---
# ── Listing card fields ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
title: "Paper Title Here"
author: "Author(s) Last Name(s)"
date: 2001                         # publication year
date-format: "YYYY"
description: "One-sentence description of identification strategy"
categories:
  - IV                             # identification: IV | OLS | RDD | DID | LATE
  - Development                    # topic tag(s)
  - "2001"                         # year as string for filtering
  - PASS                           # replication status: PASS or FAIL
image: forest_plot.png

# ── Paper metadata ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
paper-journal: "Journal Name"
paper-doi: "10.xxxx/xxxxxx"
paper-url: ""                      # optional link to paper PDF/page

# ── Replication results ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
replication-status: PASS           # PASS or FAIL
replication-delta-pct: 0.0        # max % deviation from published coefficients

# ── DML results ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dml-estimate: 0.00
dml-ci-lo: 0.00
dml-ci-hi: 0.00
dml-preferred-learner: "Random Forest"
dml-shift: "negligible"            # negligible | upward | downward | sign-change

# ── Causal Forest results (if applicable) ────────────────────────────────────
cf-estimate: null
cf-ci-lo: null
cf-ci-hi: null
cf-method: ""
cf-pct-significant: null

# ── Review process ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
rounds-completed: 1
final-verdict: "Ready"             # Ready | Minor manual revision needed | Major issues remain
---

## Paper summary

**Citation:** Author(s) (Year). *Paper Title*. *Journal*, vol(issue), pp–pp. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.xxxx/xxxxxx)

**Identification strategy:** Describe the causal identification argument in 2–3 sentences. What is the treatment variable? What instrument or design provides exogenous variation? What is the estimand (LATE, ATE, ATT)?

**Key original result:** The original paper finds [describe main result with coefficient and standard error].

---

## Replication results

The replication [passed / failed]. Maximum coefficient deviation from the published table: **X.X%**.

| Specification | Original | Replicated | Δ (%) |
|---------------|----------|------------|-------|
| Baseline OLS  | 0.00 (0.00) | 0.00 (0.00) | 0.0% |
| IV (main)     | 0.00 (0.00) | 0.00 (0.00) | 0.0% |

![Forest plot: original vs. replicated vs. DML estimates](forest_plot.png)

---

## DML Extension

Double/Debiased Machine Learning was applied using the [PLR / PLIV / IRM] model with 5-fold cross-fitting.

| Learner | Estimate | 95% CI |
|---------|----------|--------|
| LASSO   | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] |
| Random Forest | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] |
| XGBoost | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] |

**Preferred learner:** Random Forest (lowest cross-fit RMSE on nuisance functions).

**Interpretation:** The DML estimate is [negligibly different from / X% higher than / X% lower than] the original IV estimate, [supporting / qualifying] the original conclusion.

---

## Causal Forest Extension

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Causal Forest results (expand if applicable)"}

A Causal Forest (EconML `CausalForestDML` / `CausalIVForest`) was fitted to estimate individual-level treatment effects.

**ATE estimate:** [cf-estimate] ([cf-ci-lo], [cf-ci-hi])

**Share of units with statistically significant CATEs:** [cf-pct-significant]%

**GATE analysis (by quartile of predicted CATE):**

| GATE group | Mean CATE | 95% CI | N |
|------------|-----------|--------|---|
| Q1 (lowest) | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] | 0 |
| Q2 | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] | 0 |
| Q3 | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] | 0 |
| Q4 (highest) | 0.00 | [0.00, 0.00] | 0 |

**Key heterogeneity drivers:** [List top 2–3 covariates from feature importance or SHAP analysis that most strongly predict treatment effect heterogeneity.]

**Interpretation:** [Does the forest detect meaningful heterogeneity? Is the difference between Q1 and Q4 large and statistically significant? What does this tell us about who benefits most?]

:::

---

## What did causal ML add here?

[2-3 paragraphs providing an honest assessment of whether the ML extension
was informative in this context. Did it confirm or challenge the original?
What was the most useful output? What were the limitations?]

---

## Referee reports

**Referee consensus:** [1-2 sentence summary of the overall verdict and key concerns]

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Identification

{{< include reports/ref1.md >}}

## DML Methods

{{< include reports/ref2.md >}}

## Robustness

{{< include reports/ref3.md >}}

## Synthesis

{{< include reports/synthesis.md >}}

## Final Report

{{< include reports/final_report.md >}}

:::

---

## Downloads

- [Paper PDF](paper.pdf) *(add to this folder)*
- [Forest plot (PDF)](forest_plot.pdf) *(add to this folder)*
